name: Spring Vegetable Stew
recipeIngredient: 3 medium Leeks; white parts only, sliced into 1/4-inch rounds
Chopped fresh herbs: Parsley, Thyme, Basil, Marjoram
recipeInstructions:
1. Wash the sliced leeks well, as they are often sandy. Trim the artichokes (see instructions in "Artichokes and Peas" recipe). Keep them in a bowl covered with water and lemon juice.
2. Bring a large pot of water to a boil, add fava beans, and cook for about 1 minute. Remove from heat, drain, and reserve cooking water. Rinse the beans briefly under cold water; discard skins.
3. Cut off the top 3 inches of asparagus (save stalks for soup). If tips are thick, slice lengthwise. Melt butter and olive oil in a wide skillet; add leeks, artichokes, garlic, and salt.
4. Toss to coat everything with oil. Squeeze juice from second lemon over all. Cook gently without frying for several minutes.
5. Add water; simmer partially covered for about 15 minutes or until artichokes are tender but still a bit firm. Keep some liquid in the pan; add more water as needed in small increments.
6. Return reserved cooking water to heat; bring to boil. Blanch turnips, carrots, and asparagus separately, then add them along with fresh peas (add frozen peas during last 2 minutes).
7. Cook gently until all vegetables are tender and liquid reduces to a syrup. Taste for salt; adjust seasoning.
8. Stir in additional butter and chopped herbs, season with pepper.
